NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. To Jeduthun. A Psalm of David. Truly my soul silently waits for God; From Him comes my salvation.
NKJV.2: He only is my rock and my salvation; He is my defense; I shall not be greatly moved.
NKJV.3: How long will you attack a man? You shall be slain, all of you,  Like a leaning wall and a tottering fence.
NKJV.4: They only consult to cast him down from his high position; They delight in lies; They bless with their mouth, But they curse inwardly. Selah
NKJV.5: My soul, wait silently for God alone, For my expectation is from Him.
NKJV.6: He only is my rock and my salvation; He is my defense; I shall not be moved.
NKJV.7: In God is my salvation and my glory; The rock of my strength, And my refuge, is in God.
NKJV.8: Trust in Him at all times, you people; Pour out your heart before Him; God is a refuge for us. Selah
NKJV.9: Surely men of low degree are a vapor, Men of high degree are a lie; If they are weighed on the scales, They are altogether lighter than vapor.
NKJV.10: Do not trust in oppression, Nor vainly hope in robbery; If riches increase, Do not set your heart on them.
NKJV.11: God has spoken once, Twice I have heard this: That power belongs to God.
NKJV.12: Also to You, O Lord, belongs mercy; For You render to each one according to his work.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, to Jeduthun<S>3038</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. Truly my soul<S>5315</S> waiteth<S>1747</S> upon God<S>430</S>: from him cometh my salvation<S>3444</S>. <sup>Truly: or, Only</sup> <sup>waiteth: Heb. is silent</sup>
KJV.2: He only is my rock<S>6697</S> and my salvation<S>3444</S>; he is my defence<S>4869</S>; I shall not be greatly<S>7227</S> moved<S>4131</S>. <sup>defence: Heb. high place</sup>
KJV.3: How long will ye imagine mischief<S>2050</S> against a man<S>376</S>? ye shall be slain<S>7523</S> all of you: as a bowing<S>5186</S> wall<S>7023</S> shall ye be, and as a tottering<S>1760</S> fence<S>1447</S>.
KJV.4: They only consult<S>3289</S> to cast him down<S>5080</S> from his excellency<S>7613</S>: they delight<S>7521</S> in lies<S>3577</S>: they bless<S>1288</S> with their mouth<S>6310</S>, but they curse<S>7043</S> inwardly<S>7130</S>. Selah<S>5542</S>. <sup>inwardly: Heb. in their inward parts</sup>
KJV.5: My soul<S>5315</S>, wait<S>1826</S> thou only upon God<S>430</S>; for my expectation<S>8615</S> is from him.
KJV.6: He only is my rock<S>6697</S> and my salvation<S>3444</S>: he is my defence<S>4869</S>; I shall not be moved<S>4131</S>.
KJV.7: In God<S>430</S> is my salvation<S>3468</S> and my glory<S>3519</S>: the rock<S>6697</S> of my strength<S>5797</S>, and my refuge<S>4268</S>, is in God<S>430</S>.
KJV.8: Trust<S>982</S> in him at all times<S>6256</S>; ye people<S>5971</S>, pour out<S>8210</S> your heart<S>3824</S> before<S>6440</S> him: God<S>430</S> is a refuge<S>4268</S> for us. Selah<S>5542</S>.
KJV.9: Surely men<S>120</S> <S>1121</S> of low degree are vanity<S>1892</S>, and men<S>376</S> of high degree are a lie<S>3577</S>: to be laid<S>5927</S> in the balance<S>3976</S>, they are altogether<S>3162</S> lighter than vanity<S>1892</S>. <sup>altogether: or, alike</sup>
KJV.10: Trust<S>982</S> not in oppression<S>6233</S>, and become not vain<S>1891</S> in robbery<S>1498</S>: if riches<S>2428</S> increase<S>5107</S>, set<S>7896</S> not your heart<S>3820</S> upon them.
KJV.11: God<S>430</S> hath spoken<S>1696</S> once<S>259</S>; twice<S>8147</S> have I heard<S>8085</S> this<S>2098</S>; that power<S>5797</S> belongeth unto God<S>430</S>. <sup>power: or, strength</sup>
KJV.12: Also unto thee, O Lord<S>136</S>, belongeth mercy<S>2617</S>: for thou renderest<S>7999</S> to every man<S>376</S> according to his work<S>4639</S>.

ESV.1: For God alone my soul waits in silence; from him comes my salvation.
ESV.2: He alone is my rock and my salvation, my fortress; I shall not be greatly shaken.
ESV.3: How long will all of you attack a man to batter him, like a leaning wall, a tottering fence?
ESV.4: They only plan to thrust him down from his high position. They take pleasure in falsehood. They bless with their mouths, but inwardly they curse. Selah
ESV.5: For God alone, O my soul, wait in silence, for my hope is from him.
ESV.6: He only is my rock and my salvation, my fortress; I shall not be shaken.
ESV.7: On God rests my salvation and my glory; my mighty rock, my refuge is God.
ESV.8: Trust in him at all times, O people; pour out your heart before him; God is a refuge for us. Selah
ESV.9: Those of low estate are but a breath; those of high estate are a delusion; in the balances they go up; they are together lighter than a breath.
ESV.10: Put no trust in extortion; set no vain hopes on robbery; if riches increase, set not your heart on them.
ESV.11: Once God has spoken; twice have I heard this: that power belongs to God,
ESV.12: and that to you, O Lord, belongs steadfast love. For you will render to a man according to his work.

NASB.1: For the choir director; according to Jeduthun. A Psalm of David. My soul [waits] in silence for God only; From Him is my salvation.
NASB.2: He only is my rock and my salvation, My stronghold; I shall not be greatly shaken.
NASB.3: How long will you assail a man, That you may murder [him], all of you, Like a leaning wall, like a tottering fence?
NASB.4: They have counseled only to thrust him down from his high position; They delight in falsehood; They bless with their mouth, But inwardly they curse. Selah.
NASB.5: My soul, wait in silence for God only, For my hope is from Him.
NASB.6: He only is my rock and my salvation, My stronghold; I shall not be shaken.
NASB.7: On God my salvation and my glory [rest]; The rock of my strength, my refuge is in God.
NASB.8: Trust in Him at all times, O people; Pour out your heart before Him; God is a refuge for us. Selah.
NASB.9: Men of low degree are only vanity and men of rank are a lie; In the balances they go up; They are together lighter than breath.
NASB.10: Do not trust in oppression And do not vainly hope in robbery; If riches increase, do not set [your] heart [upon them].
NASB.11: Once God has spoken; Twice I have heard this: That power belongs to God;
NASB.12: And lovingkindness is Yours, O Lord, For You recompense a man according to his work.

NLT.1:
I wait quietly before God,
for my victory comes from him.
NLT.2:
He alone is my rock and my salvation,
my fortress where I will never be shaken.
NLT.3:
So many enemies against one man —
all of them trying to kill me.
To them I’m just a broken-down wall
or a tottering fence.
NLT.4:
They plan to topple me from my high position.
They delight in telling lies about me.
They praise me to my face
but curse me in their hearts. Interlude
NLT.5:
Let all that I am wait quietly before God,
for my hope is in him.
NLT.6:
He alone is my rock and my salvation,
my fortress where I will not be shaken.
NLT.7:
My victory and honor come from God alone.
He is my refuge, a rock where no enemy can reach me.
NLT.8:
O my people, trust in him at all times.
Pour out your heart to him,
for God is our refuge. Interlude
NLT.9:
Common people are as worthless as a puff of wind,
and the powerful are not what they appear to be.
If you weigh them on the scales,
together they are lighter than a breath of air.
NLT.10:
Don’t make your living by extortion
or put your hope in stealing.
And if your wealth increases,
don’t make it the center of your life.
NLT.11:
God has spoken plainly,
and I have heard it many times:
Power, O God, belongs to you;
NLT.12:
unfailing love, O Lord, is yours.
Surely you repay all people
according to what they have done.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. To Jeduthun. A Psalm by David. My soul rests in God alone. My salvation is from him.
WEB.2: He alone is my rock and my salvation, my fortress — I will never be greatly shaken.
WEB.3: How long will you assault a man, would all of you throw him down, Like a leaning wall, like a tottering fence?
WEB.4: They fully intend to throw him down from his lofty place. They delight in lies. They bless with their mouth, but they curse inwardly. Selah.
WEB.5: My soul, wait in silence for God alone, for my expectation is from him.
WEB.6: He alone is my rock and my salvation, my fortress. I will not be shaken.
WEB.7: With God is my salvation and my honor. The rock of my strength, and my refuge, is in God.
WEB.8: Trust in him at all times, you people. Pour out your heart before him. God is a refuge for us. Selah.
WEB.9: Surely men of low degree are just a breath, and men of high degree are a lie. In the balances they will go up. They are together lighter than a breath.
WEB.10: Don’t trust in oppression. Don’t become vain in robbery. If riches increase, don’t set your heart on them.
WEB.11: God has spoken once; twice I have heard this, that power belongs to God.
WEB.12: Also to you, Lord, belongs loving kindness, for you reward every man according to his work.

CSB17.1:
For the choir director: according to Jeduthun. ⓘ A psalm of David. [1] I am at rest in God alone;
my salvation comes from him. ⓙ
CSB17.2:
He alone is my rock and my salvation, ⓚ
my stronghold; I will never be shaken. ⓛ
CSB17.3:
How long will you threaten a man?
Will all of you attack [1]
as if he were a leaning wall
or a tottering fence? ⓜ
CSB17.4:
They only plan to bring him down
from his high position.
They take pleasure in lying;
they bless with their mouths,
but they curse inwardly. ⓝ Selah
CSB17.5:
Rest in God alone, my soul,
for my hope comes from him. ⓐ
CSB17.6:
He alone is my rock and my salvation, ⓑ
my stronghold; I will not be shaken. ⓒ
CSB17.7:
My salvation and glory depend on God, my strong rock.
My refuge is in God. ⓓ
CSB17.8:
Trust in him at all times, you people;
pour out your hearts before him.
God is our refuge. ⓔ Selah
CSB17.9:
Common people are only a vapor;
important people, an illusion.
Together on a scale,
they weigh less than [2] a vapor. ⓕ
CSB17.10:
Place no trust in oppression,
or false hope in robbery.
If wealth increases,
don’t set your heart on it. ⓖ
CSB17.11:
God has spoken once;
I have heard this twice:
strength belongs to God, ⓗ
CSB17.12:
and faithful love belongs to you, LORD.
For you repay each according to his works. ⓘ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; to Jeduthun [Ethan, the noted musician, founder of an official musical family]. A Psalm of David. For God alone my soul waits in silence;
From Him comes my salvation.
AMP.2:
He alone is my rock and my salvation,
My defense and my strong tower; I will not be shaken or disheartened.
AMP.3:
How long will you attack a man
So that you may murder him, all of you,
Like a leaning wall, like a tottering fence?
AMP.4:
They consult only to throw him down from his high position [to dishonor him];
They delight in lies.
They bless with [the words of] their mouths,
But inwardly they curse. Selah.
AMP.5:
For God alone my soul waits in silence and quietly submits to Him,
For my hope is from Him.
AMP.6:
He only is my rock and my salvation;
My fortress and my defense, I will not be shaken or discouraged.
AMP.7:
On God my salvation and my glory rest;
He is my rock of [unyielding] strength, my refuge is in God.
AMP.8:
Trust [confidently] in Him at all times, O people;
Pour out your heart before Him.
God is a refuge for us. Selah.
AMP.9:
Men of low degree are only a breath (emptiness), and men of [high] rank are a lie (delusion).
In the balances they go up [because they have no measurable weight or value];
They are together lighter than a breath.
AMP.10:
Do not trust in oppression,
And do not vainly hope in robbery;
If riches increase, do not set your heart on them.
AMP.11:
God has spoken once,
Twice I have heard this:
That power belongs to God.
AMP.12:
Also to You, O Lord, belong lovingkindness and compassion,
For You compensate every man according to [the value of] his work.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
God, the one and only—
I'll wait as long as he says.
Everything I need comes from him,
so why not?
MSG.2:
He's solid rock under my feet,
breathing room for my soul,
An impregnable castle:
I'm set for life.
MSG.3:
How long will you gang up on me?
How long will you run with the bullies?
There's nothing to you, any of you—
rotten floorboards, worm-eaten rafters,
MSG.4:
Anthills plotting to bring down mountains,
far gone in make-believe.
You talk a good line,
but every "blessing" breathes a curse.
MSG.5:
God, the one and only—
I'll wait as long as he says.
Everything I hope for comes from him,
so why not?
MSG.6:
He's solid rock under my feet,
breathing room for my soul,
An impregnable castle:
I'm set for life.
MSG.7:
My help and glory are in God
—granite-strength and safe-harbor-God—
MSG.8:
So trust him absolutely, people;
lay your lives on the line for him.
God is a safe place to be.
MSG.9:
Man as such is smoke,
woman as such, a mirage.
Put them together, they're nothing;
two times nothing is nothing.
MSG.10:
And a windfall, if it comes—
don't make too much of it.
MSG.11:
God said this once and for all;
how many times
Have I heard it repeated?
"Strength comes
Straight from God."
MSG.12:
Love to you, Lord God!
You pay a fair wage for a good day's work!

NIV.1:
Psalm 62
For the director of music. For Jeduthun. A psalm of David.
My soul finds rest in God alone;
my salvation comes from him.
NIV.2:
He alone is my rock and my salvation;
he is my fortress, I will never be shaken.
NIV.3:
How long will you assault a man?
Would all of you throw him down—
this leaning wall, this tottering fence?
NIV.4:
They fully intend to topple him
from his lofty place;
they take delight in lies.
With their mouths they bless,
but in their hearts they curse. Selah
NIV.5:
Find rest, O my soul, in God alone;
my hope comes from him.
NIV.6:
He alone is my rock and my salvation;
he is my fortress, I will not be shaken.
NIV.7:
My salvation and my honor depend on God;
he is my mighty rock, my refuge.
NIV.8:
Trust in him at all times, O people;
pour out your hearts to him,
for God is our refuge. Selah
NIV.9:
Lowborn men are but a breath,
the highborn are but a lie;
if weighed on a balance, they are nothing;
together they are only a breath.
NIV.10:
Do not trust in extortion
or take pride in stolen goods;
though your riches increase,
do not set your heart on them.
NIV.11:
One thing God has spoken,
two things have I heard:
that you, O God, are strong,
NIV.12:
and that you, O Lord, are loving.
Surely you will reward each person
according to what he has done.

NRSVCE.1:
For God alone my soul waits in silence;
from him comes my salvation.
NRSVCE.2:
He alone is my rock and my salvation,
my fortress; I shall never be shaken.
NRSVCE.3:
How long will you assail a person,
will you batter your victim, all of you,
as you would a leaning wall, a tottering fence?
NRSVCE.4:
Their only plan is to bring down a person of prominence.
They take pleasure in falsehood;
they bless with their mouths,
but inwardly they curse. Selah
NRSVCE.5:
For God alone my soul waits in silence,
for my hope is from him.
NRSVCE.6:
He alone is my rock and my salvation,
my fortress; I shall not be shaken.
NRSVCE.7:
On God rests my deliverance and my honor;
my mighty rock, my refuge is in God.
NRSVCE.8:
Trust in him at all times, O people;
pour out your heart before him;
God is a refuge for us. Selah
NRSVCE.9:
Those of low estate are but a breath,
those of high estate are a delusion;
in the balances they go up;
they are together lighter than a breath.
NRSVCE.10:
Put no confidence in extortion,
and set no vain hopes on robbery;
if riches increase, do not set your heart on them.
NRSVCE.11:
Once God has spoken;
twice have I heard this:
that power belongs to God,
NRSVCE.12:
and steadfast love belongs to you, O Lord.
For you repay to all
according to their work.

NET.1: For God alone I patiently wait; he is the one who delivers me.
NET.2: He alone is my protector and deliverer. He is my refuge; I will not be upended.
NET.3: How long will you threaten a man? All of you are murderers, as dangerous as a leaning wall or an unstable fence.
NET.4: They spend all their time planning how to bring him down. They love to use deceit; they pronounce blessings with their mouths, but inwardly they utter curses. (Selah)
NET.5: Patiently wait for God alone, my soul! For he is the one who gives me confidence.
NET.6: He alone is my protector and deliverer. He is my refuge; I will not be upended.
NET.7: God delivers me and exalts me; God is my strong protector and my shelter.
NET.8: Trust in him at all times, you people! Pour out your hearts before him! God is our shelter! (Selah)
NET.9: Men are nothing but a mere breath; human beings are unreliable. When they are weighed in the scales, all of them together are lighter than air.
NET.10: Do not trust in what you can gain by oppression! Do not put false confidence in what you can gain by robbery! If wealth increases, do not become attached to it!
NET.11: God has declared one principle; two principles I have heard: God is strong,
NET.12: and you, O Lord, demonstrate loyal love. For you repay men for what they do.
